text: colleagues. Recipients see the skill in the Shared with you section of their skills list. Share with organization: Members can publish a skill to the organization directory, where anyone can find and install it. Share with organization: Members can publish a skill to the organization directory, where anyone can find and install it. Both toggles are off by default. You can enable either or both in Organization settings > Skills . Note: Shared skills are view-only. Recipients can enable and use a shared skill but can't edit its contents. Group sharing and edit permissions are planned for a future release. How shared skills differ from provisioned skills Owner-provisioned Owner-provisioned Shared peer-to-peer Shared peer-to-peer Shared org-wide Shared org-wide Who can share Who can share Owners only Owners only Any member (if enabled) Any member (if enabled) Any member (if enabled) Any member (if enabled) Where it appears Where it appears Everyone's skills list Everyone's skills list Recipient's "Shared with you" section Recipient's "Shared with you" section Organization directory Organization directory Can recipients remove it? Can recipients remove it? Disable only Disable only Disable or delete Disable or delete Disable only Disable only Requires owner approval? Requires owner approval? Owner uploads directly Owner uploads directly No No No No Important: There's no approval workflow for org-wide sharing. If you enable Share with organization , any member can publish a skill to the directory without review. Consider enabling peer-to-peer sharing only if this is a concern. Monitor sharing activity Skill sharing events are captured in the audit log and Compliance API as role_assignment events. You can see who shared a skill, with whom, and whether it was peer-to-peer or organization-wide. The audit log doesn't capture the contents of shared skills—only the share event itself. There's no admin dashboard to browse or inspect the contents of skills shared between members. How members see provisioned and shared skills Skills appear for each member in Customize > Skills , organized into three sections: Personal skills: Skills the member has created or uploaded. Personal skills: Skills the member has created or uploaded. Shared with you: Skills colleagues have shared directly with a member. These appear grayed out until enabled. Shared with you: Skills colleagues have shared directly with a member. These appear grayed out until enabled. Organization skills: Skills an owner has provisioned and skills members have shared organization-wide. Members install these from the directory. Organization skills:
